title: Thelypteris neglecta
taxonomy: Thelypteris neglecta is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris and is a type of deciduous fern. The species is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It is a part of the order Polypodiales and further classified as a vascular plant, specifically an autotrophic, non-flowering plant. Thelypteris neglecta is also known as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns.
uses: Thelypteris neglecta has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, where it is believed to have various health benefits. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain.
